If this is what I think it is, staying isn't the brave choice. It's the stupid one.
I'd keep my distance. Whatever this is, it isn't getting smaller.
Nobody's been hurt yet. I keep telling myself that.
The safe move is to leave. The realistic move is to hope it passes.
Don't go poking around after dark. I mean it. Not anymore.
There's a line between caution and cowardice, and I know which side I'm on.
Whatever you do, don't go alone. That's not wisdom, that's arithmetic.
I've seen what happens to the overconfident. I'd rather be called a coward.
It's getting worse. Slowly, but the direction is clear enough.
The real danger isn't the thing itself. It's how careless people get when nothing happens for a while.
I give it a week before someone does something reckless. Maybe less.
If you're passing through, pass through quickly. That's my honest advice.
We're not equipped for this. I don't think anyone is, but especially not us.
The signs are all there if you're willing to read them. Most people aren't.
Best case, it blows over on its own. I'm not counting on best case.
